2606.21804.title: Is Agent Code Less Maintainable Than Human Code?
2606.21804.paper_summary: Maintainability is a core dimension of software engineering, shaping how code is written, reviewed, and developed over time. While coding agents have demonstrated strong performance on single-issue tasks, it remains unclear how maintainable their code is when future agents build on top of it, potentially leading to compounding downstream effects. We investigate how agent code compares to human code in these maintenance settings, presenting CodeThread, a framework to construct controlled experiments from repository-level coding benchmarks. Applying CodeThread to four frontier coding agents and four benchmarks, we find that agents are less effective at resolving tasks when building on agent code compared to human code, with task resolve rate drops of up to 13.1%. Regression analysis reveals that many traditional software engineering maintainability metrics do not explain this difference. Instead, the clearest signals are subtler behavioral differences in agent code, such as changes to input validation and error handling, along with differences in downstream code size and task difficulty. These findings highlight the need to evaluate these systems not only by immediate task resolution but also by code maintainability, and point to potential sources of downstream errors introduced by agent code.
2606.21804.paper_summary_zh: 可維護性是軟體工程的一個核心維度，影響著程式碼的撰寫、審查和隨時間的發展。雖然編碼代理在單一問題任務上表現出色，但當未來的代理在其上進行構建時，其程式碼的可維護性仍不明朗，這可能導致累積的下游影響。我們調查了代理程式碼在這些維護環境中與人類程式碼的比較，提出了 CodeThread，一個從庫級編碼基準構建受控實驗的框架。將 CodeThread 應用於四個前沿編碼代理和四個基準，我們發現當基於代理程式碼構建時，代理在解決任務方面的效果不如基於人類程式碼，任務解決率下降最高達 13.1%。回歸分析顯示，許多傳統的軟體工程可維護性指標無法解釋這一差異。相反，最明顯的信號是代理程式碼中更微妙的行為差異，例如對輸入驗證和錯誤處理的變更，以及下游程式碼大小和任務難度的差異。這些發現強調了評估這些系統時，不僅要考慮即時的任務解決，還要考慮程式碼的可維護性，並指出了代理程式碼可能引入的下游錯誤的潛在來源。
2606.21804.author: Shaswat Patel et.al.
2606.21804.authors: Shaswat Patel, Betty Li Hou, Arun Purohit, Kai Xu, Jane Pan, He He, Valerie Chen
